Neurological Medicine  ·  Monti Institute

Neurological &
Migraine Medicine

Precision neurological care delivered through structured physician-led clinical protocols.

Who We Treat

  • Chronic or episodic migraine with significant frequency or functional impact
  • Treatment-resistant headaches not adequately controlled by prior therapies
  • Complex neurologic symptom patterns requiring structured diagnostic evaluation
  • Diagnostic uncertainty cases benefiting from longitudinal physician oversight

Conditions We Treat

Chronic Migraine
Episodic Migraine
Cluster Headache
Tension-Type Headache
Neuralgia & Facial Pain
Post-Traumatic Headache

Treatment Approach

01
Longitudinal migraine management — protocols evolve with treatment response over time, not fixed at the first visit
02
Diagnostic refinement through structured reassessment — clinical decisions are revisited as new data emerges
03
Protocol adjustment based on clinical response — no patient remains on an ineffective regimen
04
Conservative escalation model — intervention intensity matches clinical necessity, not patient preference

Interventional Protocols

01
Botox for Chronic Migraine
FDA-approved OnabotulinumtoxinA protocol. 31-site injection administered every 12 weeks within a structured ongoing care plan.
02
Occipital Nerve Blocks
Targeted anesthetic injections at the greater and lesser occipital nerves for migraine rescue and cervicogenic headache.
03
Trigger Point Injections
Precision myofascial injections targeting headache amplification — integrated into a broader migraine management plan.
04
Neuromodulation Strategies
Device-based and non-pharmacologic approaches for refractory migraine when standard therapies are insufficient.
05
Infusion Therapy
Physician-supervised IV protocols: magnesium rescue, NAD+ neurological support, and adjunctive infusion therapy.
Full clinical detail available. Botox protocol, nerve block procedure, infusion compositions, CGRP therapy — complete medical specifications are in the Clinical Services section.

Neurological Care Programs

Physician-directed programs for patients requiring longitudinal neurological management. Enrollment follows clinical consultation.

Program I
Migraine Management Program
Quarterly physician-led assessment. Individualized migraine protocol updated each visit. Direct physician access between cycles.
Quarterly cycle · Annual assessment
Program II
Chronic Headache Continuity Program
Intensive longitudinal management. Recurring Botox at 12-week cycles. CGRP coordination. Expedited access for breakthrough episodes.
Monthly check-in · 12-week Botox cycles
Program III
Neurological Infusion Program
Structured IV protocols for migraine rescue, magnesium, and NAD+ support. Physician-supervised. Frequency per clinical indication.
Per clinical indication · Quarterly review
Clinical Process

Structured Care Pathway

Every neurological patient follows the same clinical sequence. No stage is skipped. No treatment is initiated ahead of the evaluation that justifies it.

01

Clinical Intake & History Review

Symptom timeline, prior treatment history, medication review, and functional impact assessment completed before any protocol is recommended.

02

Diagnostic Stratification

Headache phenotype classification per ICHD-3 criteria. Neurological burden and treatment history determine the appropriate interventional pathway.

03

Protocol Selection

Treatment is selected from the established protocol framework based on clinical findings. Patient preference informs, but does not determine, selection.

04

Longitudinal Management

Response is assessed at 12-week evaluation intervals. Protocols are adjusted in response to clinical data. Care is continuous, not episodic.

Clinical Standards
  • No treatment is initiated without a structured clinical evaluation. The first visit establishes the clinical picture; protocol selection follows from it.
  • Escalation is governed by clinical response, not patient preference or scheduling convenience. Conservative sequencing is the default.
  • Restraint is a clinical decision. What we decline to offer is as defining as what we provide.

Advanced Facial Therapeutics  ·  Monti Institute

Aesthetic
Medicine

Structural precision aesthetics within a physician-led institute — injectables, energy-based devices, and regenerative care.

Who This Is For

  • Patients seeking subtle, natural aesthetic enhancement — not visible transformation
  • Structural refinement through conservative, anatomically guided treatment
  • Physician-guided care with documented clinical reasoning at each visit
  • Long-term aesthetic planning mindset — outcomes built gradually over time
Treatment Categories

Aesthetic Medicine Systems

Neuromodulators
Dermal Fillers
Laser & Energy Devices
Skin Rejuvenation
Regenerative Aesthetics
Body Contouring

Aesthetic Approach

01
Conservative, buildable results — treatments are staged incrementally, never delivered beyond what the tissue acceptably absorbs
02
Anatomically guided treatment planning — all injection and device protocols are governed by three-dimensional facial structure, not trend
03
Facial balance over volume — structural harmony and proportional integrity are prioritized above any individual feature
04
Long-term aesthetic evolution — each treatment is considered within the patient's aging trajectory, not as an isolated event

Aesthetic Protocols

01
Full-Face Rebalancing
Comprehensive injectable approach addressing multiple zones simultaneously. Structural balance, not surface correction.
02
Lip Enhancement
Precise hyaluronic acid placement for natural lip structure and symmetry. Never overcorrected, always proportionate.
03
Jawline & Chin Contouring
Lower face definition through strategic filler and neuromodulator placement — sharpening contour without altering identity.
04
Skin Tightening & Resurfacing
Energy-based and fractional laser protocols for collagen stimulation, surface refinement, and non-surgical tissue lifting.
05
Collagen-Stimulating Therapies
Sculptra and biostimulatory agents rebuilding dermal architecture progressively. Results emerge over months, not immediately.
06
Pigment & Vascular Correction
Pico, IPL, and Nd:YAG systems for targeted correction of sun damage, melasma, vascular lesions, and acne discoloration.
Full treatment detail available. Injectable protocols, device specifications, laser parameters, and regenerative treatment logic — complete clinical detail in the Clinical Services section.

Aesthetic Care Programs

Physician-supervised programs for sustained aesthetic care — planned across time and designed around long-term skin and facial health. Enrollment requires an initial consultation.

Program I
Preventive Aesthetic Care
Quarterly facial assessment, conservative injectable planning, and skin health monitoring. Annual comprehensive review.
Quarterly assessment · Annual review
Program II
Facial Optimization Program
Integrated injectable and device-based treatment planning. Staged rebalancing across zones. 12-month aesthetic horizon.
Quarterly full review · 12-month horizon
Program III
Regenerative Skin Health
Laser and collagen stimulation staging. Long-term dermal remodeling and resurfacing. Diagnostic skin mapping each cycle.
Monthly or quarterly cycles · Annual protocol
Clinical Process

Structured Treatment Pathway

Every aesthetic treatment plan at the Monti Institute follows a defined clinical sequence. Assessment precedes protocol selection. Evaluation precedes escalation.

01

Anatomical Assessment

Three-dimensional facial mapping — skeletal support, fat compartment boundaries, SMAS-layer integrity, and dermal quality — conducted before any protocol is designed.

02

Protocol Design

Treatment is designed from assessment findings, not selected from a menu. Modality, sequencing, and dosing are individualized to the patient's specific anatomical indication.

03

Staged Execution

Treatments are sequenced across defined intervals. Each cycle builds on tissue response data from the prior cycle. Results are evaluated against documented baseline.

04

12-Month Horizon Review

Structural outcomes are assessed at 6 and 12 months against enrollment baseline. The forward protocol for the next cycle is designed from that comparative data.

Clinical Standards
  • No procedure is initiated without documented clinical indication. Treatment is justified by anatomical assessment, not patient preference alone.
  • We do not overcorrect. Volume, neuromodulation, and structural refinement are calibrated to anatomy — not maximized to request.
  • The aesthetic outcomes we decline to produce are as defining as those we pursue.

The Institute

About the Monti Institute

A physician-led institute in Reno, Nevada, providing neurological medicine and advanced facial therapeutics through two distinct clinical divisions under unified oversight.

Mission

Integrated Precision Medicine for Neurological & Aesthetic Wellness

The Monti Institute was founded on the clinical observation that neurological health and facial structural integrity share biological pathways — and that managing both within a unified physician-directed framework produces outcomes neither discipline achieves in isolation.

Clinical Philosophy

How We Practice

01
Systems-Based Diagnostics
Every encounter begins with a complete clinical evaluation. No intervention is initiated before the full picture is established.
02
Protocol-Driven Care
Every protocol is designed around the individual clinical profile. No templated plans. Protocols evolve with clinical response.
03
Conservative Execution
Intervention is calibrated to clinical necessity. We do not escalate ahead of evidence. Long-term structural and neurological health governs every decision.
04
Advanced Diagnostics & Regenerative Aesthetics
Advanced imaging, tissue analysis, and biological markers inform treatment selection — grounding aesthetic and neurological decisions in measurable clinical data.

Nothing is accidental. Everything is intentional.

Clinical Divisions
Division I
Neurological Medicine

Diagnosis and management of migraine, headache disorders, and neurologic pain. Covered by select major insurance providers.

Division II
Advanced Facial Therapeutics

Injectables, energy-based devices, and regenerative treatments delivered within a structured clinical framework. Private-pay.

Monti Institute  ·  Treatment Credits

The Gift of
Precision Care

Private Treatment Credits allow recipients to access aesthetic and neurological consultations at the Monti Institute — with physician oversight and clinical appropriateness confirmed at activation.

Requires physician evaluation prior to activation. No treatment is guaranteed without clinical assessment.

What This Is

Not a Gift Card.
A Clinical Access Credit.

Private Treatment Credits are not spa vouchers. They are discretionary access instruments for evidence-based aesthetic medicine and physician-led neurological care — to be activated through consultation and applied with clinical intention.

Credit Tiers

Select a Treatment Credit

$100
Consultation Credit
Applies toward initial consultation or skin assessment visit
$250
Treatment Credit
Applicable toward injectable or skin rejuvenation protocols
$500
Protocol Credit
Designed for multi-modality or device-based treatment plans
$1,000+
Regenerative Credit
Full protocol access — applied toward comprehensive regenerative plans

Requires physician evaluation prior to activation. No treatment is guaranteed without clinical assessment. Credits are non-refundable and applied at the physician's clinical discretion. Not redeemable for cash.

How It Works

Clinical Activation Process

01
Credit Requested
Contact the institute to request a Private Treatment Credit in the desired amount. Fulfillment is confirmed within 48 hours.
02
Consultation Booked
The recipient schedules their initial consultation. A physician reviews clinical appropriateness before any treatment is recommended.
03
Credit Applied
Following physician approval, the credit is applied toward the agreed treatment protocol. No treatment proceeds without clinical confirmation.

“Every credit activates through consultation. Every treatment requires physician oversight. This is clinical care — gifted with intention.”

Request a Credit

Request a Private Treatment Credit

Contact our team to arrange a Private Treatment Credit for yourself or on behalf of someone in your care. Fulfillment is handled discretely and confirmed within 48 business hours.

/ Clinical Services
Neurological Medicine  ·  Clinical Detail

Interventional & Therapeutic
Protocols

Full clinical detail for all neurological interventions offered at the Monti Institute. Treatment selection is determined by diagnostic evaluation, not patient preference.

Interventional Protocols
01
FDA-Approved Prevention
Botox for Chronic Migraine

OnabotulinumtoxinA administered at 31 standardized injection sites across the head and neck. Indicated for chronic migraine (15+ headache days per month). Protocol follows FDA-approved dosing and site mapping.

  • Administered every 12 weeks as part of a structured ongoing care plan
  • 155–195 units distributed across 7 head and neck muscle groups
  • Response typically assessed after 2 full treatment cycles
  • Candidates: chronic migraine with 4+ hours of headache duration per day
02
Interventional Block
Occipital Nerve Blocks

Targeted anesthetic injection at the greater and lesser occipital nerves. Used for migraine rescue, occipital neuralgia, and cervicogenic headache. Provides rapid onset relief with minimal systemic effect.

  • Local anesthetic ± corticosteroid per clinical indication
  • Can be performed as standalone or adjunct to Botox protocol
  • Repeated as clinically indicated — no fixed minimum interval
  • Effective for occipital neuralgia, cervicogenic headache, cluster headache
03
Myofascial
Trigger Point Injections

Precision injections targeting myofascial trigger points that contribute to headache amplification. Integrated into a broader migraine management plan rather than used as standalone treatment.

  • Sites: suboccipital, trapezius, temporalis, sternocleidomastoid
  • Anesthetic injection with optional dry needling protocol
  • Session typically addresses 3–6 active trigger points
  • Most effective when combined with pharmacologic management
04
Non-Pharmacologic
Neuromodulation Strategies

Device-based and non-pharmacologic approaches for patients with refractory migraine, medication overuse headache, or pharmacologic contraindications. Complements interventional and pharmacologic protocols.

  • External trigeminal nerve stimulation (Cefaly-class devices)
  • Remote electrical neuromodulation protocols
  • TENS-based adjunct therapy for chronic pain amplification
  • Integrated into care plan alongside medication optimization
05
IV Infusion
Neurological Infusion Therapy

Physician-supervised intravenous protocols for acute migraine rescue, neurological support, and adjunctive therapy. Infusion composition is individualized and adjusted based on clinical response and indication.

  • Magnesium sulfate: acute migraine rescue and prevention
  • NAD+: neurological energy support and mitochondrial function
  • DHE infusion: severe refractory migraine protocol
  • Ketorolac + antiemetic adjunct: standard acute relief protocol
  • Frequency per clinical indication — not a fixed subscription
06
Preventive Pharmacology
CGRP Pathway Therapies

Calcitonin gene-related peptide (CGRP) monoclonal antibody therapies represent the most significant advance in migraine prevention in decades. Prescribed and managed as part of a structured longitudinal protocol.

  • Monthly or quarterly subcutaneous injection per medication class
  • Erenumab (Aimovig), Fremanezumab (Ajovy), Galcanezumab (Emgality)
  • Indicated for episodic and chronic migraine with inadequate response to prior preventives
  • Response assessed at 3 months; protocol adjusted accordingly

/ Clinical Services
Advanced Facial Therapeutics  ·  Clinical Detail

Aesthetic Treatment
Protocols

Full clinical detail for aesthetic interventions offered at the Monti Institute. All treatments are physician-supervised and selected following clinical assessment.

Injectable Protocols
01
Neuromodulators
Botox & Dysport

Precision neuromodulator placement for dynamic wrinkle reduction and facial balance. All injection mapping is governed by three-dimensional anatomical assessment — not templated dosing patterns.

  • Forehead, glabella, crow's feet — primary zones
  • Brow lift, lip lines, mentalis, platysma — secondary refinement
  • Masseter reduction for jawline slimming and bruxism
  • Results onset 3–5 days, peak at 2 weeks, duration 3–4 months
  • Conservative initial dosing; titrated at follow-up assessment
02
Structural Restoration
Dermal Fillers

Hyaluronic acid filler placement for structural volumization, contour refinement, and facial balance. Protocol prioritizes anatomy over augmentation — restoring structure, not adding volume.

  • Cheek and midface: structural support and volume restoration
  • Nasolabial and perioral: softening without overfilling
  • Lip enhancement: natural structure and symmetry, not augmentation
  • Jawline and chin: lower face definition and contour
  • Tear trough: under-eye hollowing correction with appropriate product selection
03
Biostimulatory
Collagen-Stimulating Therapies

Sculptra (poly-L-lactic acid) and other biostimulatory agents that rebuild dermal architecture progressively. Results emerge over 3–6 months rather than immediately — the preferred approach for natural, long-duration outcomes.

  • Sculptra: serial treatment series of 2–3 sessions spaced 6–8 weeks
  • Radiesse: immediate + biostimulatory dual-action filler
  • Most suitable for volume loss, skin laxity, and long-term facial rejuvenation
  • Not recommended for superficial lines or lip enhancement
Laser & Energy-Based Devices
04
Resurfacing
Laser Resurfacing & Skin Renewal

Fractional and ablative laser protocols for skin texture refinement, pigmentation correction, and collagen remodeling. Device and parameter selection is individualized based on Fitzpatrick type, skin condition, and target concern.

  • Fractional CO2 and Erbium: deep resurfacing for texture and scarring
  • Non-ablative fractional: minimal downtime collagen stimulation
  • Protocol pacing governed by tissue recovery — not scheduling preference
  • Series of 2–4 treatments typically recommended for optimal outcome
05
Pigment & Vascular
Pico · IPL · Nd:YAG

Targeted correction of pigmentation irregularities, vascular lesions, and skin tone unevenness. Multi-wavelength systems allow precise targeting without thermal injury to surrounding tissue.

  • PicoWay/PicoSure: pigmentation, melasma, tattoo reduction
  • IPL (intense pulsed light): diffuse redness, sun damage, rosacea
  • Nd:YAG 1064nm: deeper vascular lesions, leg veins, resistant pigment
  • Combination approach often required for complex skin conditions
06
Lifting & Tightening
Radiofrequency & Skin Tightening

Radiofrequency and ultrasound-based devices for non-surgical tissue lifting and dermal remodeling. Indicated for skin laxity, jowling, and early ptosis where surgical intervention is not indicated or desired.

  • Morpheus8 (RF microneedling): deep dermal remodeling + surface resurfacing
  • Ultherapy/HIFU: SMAS-level lifting for lower face and neck
  • Thermage: broad-surface collagen contraction for skin tightening
  • Results develop over 3–6 months as collagen remodeling progresses
07
Contouring
Body Contouring

Non-surgical body contouring using cryolipolysis, radiofrequency, and electromagnetic muscle stimulation. Results are incremental and require appropriate patient selection — not a substitute for weight loss.

  • CoolSculpting/cryolipolysis: targeted fat reduction in stubborn areas
  • RF body: skin tightening alongside volume reduction
  • Emsculpt-class: muscle definition and diastasis recti improvement
  • Suitable for patients within 20–30 lbs of goal weight with localized concerns
